name: Earned Value Management
summary: A programme-control framework that compares planned work, completed work, and actual cost to show whether an aerospace programme is ahead, behind, or over budget.
whenToUseIt: Use it on multi-team engineering programmes where schedule slip or cost growth can hide behind partial technical progress.
steps: Define the work breakdown structure and budgeted value for each package.
Track planned value for the current reporting period.
Measure earned value from verified completed work.
Compare earned value to actual cost and planned value.
Use the resulting variances and performance indices to trigger management action.
example: If an avionics integration package is 60 percent complete but has spent 85 percent of its budget, earned value reveals the problem early enough to re-sequence work or add technical support before it derails the full programme.
